```{r partisanAMCEcancel, fig.cap="Support for cancelation, Subset by party, AMCE"}
  #republicans,
  #cancel_formula,
  #id = ~ID
#) %>% mutate(Group= "Republicans")

#democrats <- filter(conjoint,Democrat==1)

#dems_cancel <- amce(
  #democrats,
  #cancel_formula,
  #id = ~ID
#) %>% mutate(Group="Democrats")

#cancel_partisan <- rbind(republicans_responsible,dems_responsible)

#modelplot_multi(cancel_partisan, subset = TRUE)

```

```{r }
library(kableExtra)


#custom_plot(full_sample_model_resp) + 
  #labs(title  = "Debtor Responsible, Full Sample",caption = responsibility_caption)

#save this for later renders to pdf or html
#kable(full_sample_model_resp, "html") %>%
  #kable_styling(font_size = 12, position = "center") %>%
  #row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = "#4F81BD")
#knitr::kable(full_sample_model_resp[1:10, ], caption = "Do debtors bear responsiibility? AMCE, Full Sample")

#I'm gonna be doing this a bunch so this is a function for making the tables look good

nice_table_true <- function(df,group=FALSE){
  
  df2 <- df %>% 
    #removing columns that don't make sense
    select(-outcome,-statistic,-feature) %>%
    #removing blank rows
    filter(estimate!=0) %>% 
    #creating Better labels
    mutate(Characteristic=case_when(
      level=="Just Cancelled"~"Neutral framing of cancelation (relative to partisan)",
      level=="Grad School"~"Grad School (relative to First Gen student)",
      level=="Finished"~"Finished Degree (relative to uncompleted)",
      level=="More likely to be White"~"Majority white (relative to majority non-white)",
      level=="Working"~"Working (relative to unemployed)",
      TRUE~NA
    )) %>% select(-level) %>% 
  mutate(P = paste0(p, 
                    ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
                    ifelse(p < 0.01, "**"
                    #ifelse(p < 0.05, "*"
                    #ifelse(p < 0.1, "+", "",
                           #)
                    )))) 
    #reordering so that the characteristic column is there
    if(group==TRUE){
      #if it's a subgroup graph
       df3 <- df2 %>% 
         mutate(Group=BY) %>% 
         select(-BY) %>% 
       select(Characteristic,everything())

      
    }
  
  else{
    df3 <- df2 %>% 
       select(Characteristic,everything())

    
    
  }
    
    return(df3)
    
  
  
  
}

#claude version below
nice_table_true <- function(df, group=FALSE) {
  df2 <- df %>%
    select(-outcome, -statistic, -feature) %>%
    filter(estimate != 0) %>%
    mutate(Characteristic = case_when(
      level == "Just Cancelled" ~ "Neutral framing of cancelation (relative to partisan)",
      level == "Grad School" ~ "Grad School (relative to First Gen student)",
      level == "Finished" ~ "Finished Degree (relative to uncompleted)",
      level == "More likely to be White" ~ "Majority white (relative to majority non-white)",
      level == "Working" ~ "Working (relative to unemployed)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )) %>%
    select(-level) %>%
    mutate(estimate = case_when(
      p < 0.001 ~ paste0(estimate, "***"),
      p < 0.01 ~ paste0(estimate, "**"),
      p < 0.05 ~ paste0(estimate, "*"),
      #p < 0.1 ~ paste0(estimate, "+"),
      TRUE ~ as.character(estimate)
    ))

  if (group == TRUE) {
    df3 <- df2 %>%
      mutate(Group = BY) %>%
      select(-BY) %>%
      select(Characteristic, estimate, everything()) %>%
      arrange(desc(as.numeric(estimate)))
  } else {
    df3 <- df2 %>%
      select(Characteristic, estimate, everything()) %>%
      arrange(desc(as.numeric(estimate)))
  }

  return(df3)
}#test_republicans_cancel %>% nice_table() %>% 
  #kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  #kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))

```

```{r}

#this version of the function is for tables with no partisan column
process_dataframe_np <- function(df) {
  processed_df <- df %>%
    arrange(desc(estimate)) %>% 
    select(-statistic, -feature, -outcome) %>%
    mutate(
      # good labels for variables
      Characteristic = case_when(
        level == "Just Cancelled" ~ "Neutral framing of cancelation (relative to partisan)",
        level == "Grad School" ~ "Grad School (relative to First Gen student)",
        level == "Finished" ~ "Finished Degree (relative to uncompleted)",
        level == "More likely to be White" ~ "Majority white (relative to majority non-white)",
        level == "Working" ~ "Working (relative to unemployed)",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      
      # combining with partisanship
      good_label = Characteristic,
      
      # making good estimate column
      # with stars
      Significance = case_when(
        p < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p < 0.05 ~ "*",
        p < 0.1 ~ "+",
        TRUE ~ ""
      ),
      
      EstimateWithSymbol = paste0(round(estimate, 3), Significance),
      
      good_estimate = paste(EstimateWithSymbol, "\n(", round(std.error, 3), ")", sep = ""),
      
      good_upper=round(upper,3),
      
      good_lower=round(lower,3)

    ) %>%
    filter(estimate != 0) %>%
    select(-estimate, -z, , -level, , -Significance, -EstimateWithSymbol, -Characteristic, -std.error,-lower,-upper) %>%
    # renaming to be more aesthetically pleasing
    mutate(
      Characteristic = good_label,
      estimate = good_estimate,
       lower=good_lower,
      upper=good_upper

    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Characteristic, estimate,lower,upper)
  
explanation_row <- data.frame(
    Characteristic = "+ p<0.1, * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001",
    estimate = ""
  )  #%>%
    #arrange(desc(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]", "", estimate))))
  
  result_df <- bind_rows(processed_df, explanation_row)
  
  #removing ugly NA    
  result_df[is.na(result_df)] <-" "

  return(result_df)

   
   
}

```

```{r tableAMCEresp}
full_sample_model_resp <- amce(
  conjoint,
  debtors_not_responsible ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working,
  id = ~ID
) 


display_table <- process_dataframe_np(full_sample_model_resp)

#
display_table %>% 
  kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE,caption = "Full Sample, AMCE, Debtor responsibility outcome ") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))

 

```

```{r}
full_sample_model_resp <- amce(
  conjoint,
  debt_cancel ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working,
  id = ~ID
) 


display_table <- process_dataframe_np(full_sample_model_resp)

#
display_table %>% 
  kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE,caption = "Full Sample, AMCE, Debt cancelation outcome ") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))

```

```{r}
#this is a function to make the partisanship tables neat
# Load the dplyr package

# Define the function
process_dataframe <- function(df) {
  processed_df <- df %>%
    arrange(desc(estimate)) %>% 
    select(-statistic, -feature, -outcome) %>%
    mutate(
      # good labels for variables
      Characteristic = case_when(
        level == "Just Cancelled" ~ "Neutral framing of cancelation (relative to partisan)",
        level == "Grad School" ~ "Grad School (relative to First Gen student)",
        level == "Finished" ~ "Finished Degree (relative to uncompleted)",
        level == "More likely to be White" ~ "Majority white (relative to majority non-white)",
        level == "Working" ~ "Working (relative to unemployed)",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      
      # combining with partisanship
      good_label = paste0(Characteristic, ", ", BY),
      
      # making good estimate column
      # with stars
      Significance = case_when(
        p < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p < 0.05 ~ "*",
        p < 0.1 ~ "+",
        TRUE ~ ""
      ),
      
      EstimateWithSymbol = paste0(round(estimate, 3), Significance),
      
      good_estimate = paste(EstimateWithSymbol, "\n(", round(std.error, 3), ")", sep = ""),
      
      good_upper=round(upper,3),
      
      good_lower=round(lower,3)
    ) %>%
    filter(estimate != 0) %>%
    select(-estimate, -z, -Partisan, -level, -BY, -Significance, -EstimateWithSymbol, -Characteristic, -std.error,-upper,-lower) %>%
    # renaming to be more aesthetically pleasing
    mutate(
      Characteristic = good_label,
      estimate = good_estimate,
      lower=good_lower,
      upper=good_upper
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Characteristic, estimate,lower,upper)
  
explanation_row <- data.frame(
    Characteristic = "+ p<0.1, * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001",
    estimate = ""
  )  #%>%
    #arrange(desc(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]", "", estimate))))
  
  result_df <- bind_rows(processed_df, explanation_row)
  
  #removing ugly NA    
  result_df[is.na(result_df)] <-" "

  return(result_df)

   
   
}

# Example usage
# Assuming df is your input dataframe
# df_processed <- process_dataframe(df)


```

```{r}
mm_by <- cj(conjoint, debtors_not_responsible ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "amce", by = ~Partisan) %>% filter(Partisan!="Other/Indepdent")

display_table <- process_dataframe(mm_by)


display_table  %>% 
  kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE,caption = "Partisan Subsetting, AMCE, Debtor responsibility outcome") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))


```

```{r}
mm_by <- cj(conjoint, debt_cancel ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "amce", by = ~Partisan) %>% filter(Partisan!="Other/Indepdent")


display_table <- process_dataframe(mm_by)


display_table  %>% 
  kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE,caption = "Partisan Subsetting, AMCE, Debt Cancelation Outcome") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))


```

```{r}
test <- mm_by %>% select(-statistic,-feature,-outcome) %>% 
  mutate(
    
    #good labels for variables
    Characteristic = case_when(
      level == "Just Cancelled" ~ "Neutral framing of cancelation (relative to partisan)",
      level == "Grad School" ~ "Grad School (relative to First Gen student)",
      level == "Finished" ~ "Finished Degree (relative to uncompleted)",
      level == "More likely to be White" ~ "Majority white (relative to majority non-white)",
      level == "Working" ~ "Working (relative to unemployed)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    #combining with partisanship
    good_label= paste0(Characteristic,", ",BY),
    
    #making good estimate column
    
    #with stars
    
    Significance = case_when(
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01 ~ "**",
    p < 0.05 ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ ""
  ),
  
  EstimateWithSymbol = paste0(round(estimate, 3), Significance),

    
    good_estimate = paste(EstimateWithSymbol, "\n(", round(std.error, 3), ")", sep = ""),
  

    ) %>% 
  
filter (estimate !=0) %>% select(-estimate,-z,-p,-Partisan,-level,-BY,-Significance,-EstimateWithSymbol,-Characteristic,-std.error) %>% 
  #renaming to be more asethetically pleasing
   mutate(
    
    Characteristic= good_label,
    
    estimate = good_estimate) %>% dplyr::select(Characteristic,estimate,lower,upper)
  
  
```

## Discussion

So, are student debtors perceived as privileged dilettantes selfishly asking for a handout, or victims of circumstances beyond their control? Overall, my findings are mixed. The most significant results are that subjects are generally more sympathetic towards debtors when they are perceived as being first generation students (but not more willing to cancel debt), and that among Republicans the race of debtors impacts willingness to support cancellation. I discuss these results in more detail below.

There is evidence from the CES module for *H1*, the greater need hypothesis. To hold the characteristics that lead to high or low guessing constant, I subset the sample by those who guessed over and those who guessed under the actual percent of first-generation student debtors. Among those who guessed under the "actual" percentage, receiving an information treatment (and thus learning that debtors were potentially a more economically precarious group than they had assumed) was positively associated with reporting warmer feelings towards debtors as a group at statistically significant levels, both with and without covariates. This suggests that seeing debtors as more economically precarious/less privileged engenders more sympathetic attitudes towards them. However, that same module also found that receiving this treatment did *not* lead to more support for debt cancellation, suggesting that while attitudes towards debtors may be impacted by seeing them as more needy, policy preferences regarding them are not.

The evidence from the conjoint experiment was similarly mixed in terms of *H1* and *H2* (the better conduct hypothesis). In terms of preference description, for both outcomes the marginal means mostly showed more preference for characteristics that signaled better conduct (being employed and finishing degree program) rather than ones that signaled greater need. But the differences, both overall and among subgroups, were mostly minor, and the marginal means for being a first generation student were higher for both outcomes. Causally, the AMCE of these characteristics was similarly mixed, with clear evidence for neither hypothesis.

In terms of *H3*, the group attitudes hypotheses, there was some evidence that group attitudes did in fact impact both attitudes towards debtors and willingness to support debt cancellation. Across both outcomes and all subgroups, the marginal means were higher for subjects primed to see debtors as being majority white, compared to majority non-white (albeit very slightly in most cases). Causally, among Republican subjects, race was the only characteristic that appeared to meaningfully impact willingness to support student debt cancellation, with more (less) willingness to support cancellation for white (non-white) debtors.

Finally, I find relatively little evidence for *H4,* the partisan attitudes hypotheses. Partisan framing, both in terms of preference description and causally, did not appear to meaningfully impact either outcome. The one notable exception here is that for the debtor responsibility outcome, marginal means were notably higher for those exposed to a neutral framing of cancellation as opposed to a partisan one..among *Democrats.* While I find relatively little evidence that partisan framing meaningfully impacted outcomes I do find strong evidence that attitudes towards both student debtors and debt cancellation are a priori relatively polarized along partisan lines in terms of preferences.

More broadly, this study finds that student debtors fit into the existing literature on deservingness and welfare attitudes only partially. Consistent with frameworks like CARIN, priming subjects to see debtors as a less privileged group leads some to express warmer feelings and more sympathy towards debtors. However, this increased sympathy does not necessarily translate into greater willingness to support student debt cancellation policies. The results also replicate previous findings that Republicans are less willing to support debt relief for non-white debtors, suggesting racial attitudes play a role. Overall, this research makes an important contribution as the first systematic investigation into how student debtors are socially constructed and perceived in the popular imagination. This advances the literature on deservingness and welfare attitudes by examining how they impact a group that is less unambiguously marginalized compared to traditional welfare recipients.

There are several implications here. First, this serves as a (partial) validation of previous work that manipulated characteristics of individual debtors [@sorelleDeservingnessPoliticsStudent2023; @sorellePoliticalBenefitsStudent2023]. By instead manipulating the characteristics of debtors as a group, I preserve external validity. Unlike this previous work, I do not find any causal effect of characteristics that indicate higher levels of need, such as first-generation status, on either attitudes towards debtors. I do, however, replicate their findings that Republicans are less willing to support debt cancellation for non-white debtors, which is consistent with earlier work on racial attitudes and support for welfare policies.

More broadly, the way in which these findings do not neatly fit the existing literature on the subject means that more work is needed to broaden our understandings of how more socially ambiguous groups such as debtors fit into these frameworks. The findings of this study open up several promising avenues for future research in this area. One potential direction would be to examine the impact of the administration's attempted 2022 student debt cancellation on political attitudes and behaviors, not just for non-debtors, but for student loan borrowers themselves. This could involve conducting survey experiments to isolate the effects of the cancellation attempt on various political outcomes. Additionally, researchers could analyze observational data to track changes in political engagement, voting patterns, or policy preferences among both groups over time.

\newpage

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

\newpage

# Appendix

```         
```

```{r}
setwd("~/Desktop/Grad School/CES 2022")
#this is the matched data 
cces <- read_dta("CCES22_UCR_OUTPUT.DTA")
setwd("/Users/alexanderross/Desktop/Grad School/debt_conjoint 2")

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-groupchart
#| fig-cap: "Percentage of Student Debtors that subjects guessed were first generation college students, by subgroup. Dashed line is at 52% (actual percent)."# Pivot longer to get a single category column

#pacman::p_load(tidyverse,DiagrammeR,tidygraph,ggraph,DiagrammeRsvg,rsvg,magrittr,janitor,egg,ggpubr,haven,data.table,modelsummary,naniar,scales,gridExtra,readr,reshape2,hrbrthemes,patchwork,wesanderson,ggthemes,gridExtra)

long_data <- cces_clean %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Partisanship, Gender, Race,Education, Debt), names_to = "Category", values_to = "Value") %>%
  group_by(Category, Value) %>%
  summarise(AverageDebt = mean(debt_guess,na.rm = TRUE), 
            DebtSD = sd(debt_guess, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = 'drop') 

# Plotting
ggplot(long_data, aes(x = Value, y = AverageDebt, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = AverageDebt - DebtSD, ymax = AverageDebt + DebtSD),
                position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.2) +
  scale_color_economist()+theme_tufte()+
  labs(title = NULL,
       x = "Group",
       y = "Average guess by group, with confidence intervals",
       fill = "Category") +
theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), # Rotate bottom labels
        legend.position = "none") + # Remove legend
  facet_grid(~Category, scales = "free_x")+ # Facets for each category
  geom_hline(yintercept = 52, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

```{r}
cces_clean <- cces %>% 
  filter(UCR343 !=8,) %>%
  mutate(treated=case_when(QUCR341rand==1~1,
                           TRUE~0))%>% 
  mutate(control=case_when(QUCR341rand==2~1,
                           TRUE~0))%>% 
  mutate(debt_guess=UCR340) %>% 
  mutate(debtor_therm=UCR342) %>%
  #mutate(debt_relief=10-UCR343) %>% 
  mutate(therm_first=case_when(QUCR342rand==1~1,TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(relief_first=case_when(QUCR342rand==2~1,TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(student_debtor=case_when(edloan==1~1,TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(treat_difference=case_when(control==1~0,
                                    treated==1~52-debt_guess),
         treat_guess_high=case_when(treated==1&debt_guess>52~1,TRUE~0),
         treat_guess_low=case_when(treated==1&debt_guess<52~1,TRUE~0),
         high_guess=case_when(debt_guess>52~1,TRUE~0),
         low_guess=case_when(debt_guess<52~1,TRUE~0),
         treat_size=abs(treat_difference),
         treat_large=case_when(treat_difference>=10~1, TRUE~0),
         treat_small=case_when(treat_difference<=10~1, TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(female = case_when(
    gender4 ==2~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(not.white=case_when(
    race >= 2~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(reg.democrat=case_when(
    CC22_360== 2~1,
    TRUE~0
  )) %>% 
  mutate(reg.republican=case_when(
    CC22_360==3~1,
    TRUE~0
  )) %>% #replacing not sure and don't know with NAs, party id scale
  mutate(dem_to_rep=pid7) %>% 
  mutate(college.grad=case_when(
    educ>=4~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(age=(2022-birthyr)) %>% 
  mutate(urban=case_when(
    urbancity==1~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(rural=case_when(
    urbancity==4~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(some.coll=case_when(
    educ==3~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>%
  mutate(two.yr.degree=case_when(
    educ==4~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>%
  mutate(four.yr.degree=case_when(
    educ==5~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>%
  mutate(post.grad=case_when(
    educ==6~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(student.debtor=case_when(
    edloan==1~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  mutate(military=case_when(
    milstat_1==1~1,
    milstat_3==1~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>%
  mutate(lost_job=case_when(
    CC22_305_2==1~1,
    TRUE~0)) %>% 
  replace_with_na("ideo5", 6) %>% 
  filter(UCR343!=8) %>% 
  mutate(debt_relief=UCR343) %>% 
  #variables for the graph
  mutate(
    
    Partisanship=case_when(pid7 %in% c(1,2,3)~"Democrat",
                           pid7 %in% c(5,6,7)~"Republican", pid7 %in% c(4,8,9)~"Independent"
    ),
    
    Race= case_when(not.white==1~"Non White",
                    TRUE~"White"),
    
    Debt=case_when(edloan==1~"Debtor",
                   TRUE~"Non Debtor"),
    
    Education=case_when(educ>=4~"At least some college",
                        TRUE~"No College"),
    
    Gender=case_when(female==1~"Female",TRUE~"Male"),
    
    `News Interest`=case_when(newsint%in% c(1,2)~"High Interest",TRUE~"Low Interest")
    
  ) #%>% filter(debt_guess!=52&debt_guess!=50)
#cces_clean %>% filter(Partisanship=="Democrat") %>% nrow()



high_guessers <- filter(cces_clean,high_guess==1)
low_guessers <- filter(cces_clean,low_guess==1)


high_guess_dems <- filter(high_guessers,pid7%in% c(1,2,3))
low_guess_dems <- filter(low_guessers,pid7%in% c(1,2,3))

high_guess_gop <- filter(high_guessers,pid7 %in% c(5,6,8))
low_guess_gop <- filter(low_guessers,pid7 %in%  c(5,6,8))



models <- list(
  
  "All"=lm(debtor_therm~treated*relief_first,data=cces_clean),
  
  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~treated*relief_first,data=high_guessers),
  
  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~treated*relief_first,data=low_guessers),
  
  
  "All"=lm(debtor_therm~treated,data=cces_clean),
  
  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~treated,data=high_guessers),
  
  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~treated,data=low_guessers)
  
  
)

modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Feeling thermometer towards debtors as a group (Matched Data)",
             
             coef_rename =coef_rename, notes=note_add("coded 1-100, higher values represent warmer feelings")
             
) %>% add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3)) %>% kable_styling(latex_options="scale_down", "striped")

```

```{r tabdebthtermcovars}
models <- list(
  
  "All"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = high_guessers),

  
    "All"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = high_guessers)
  
)

 modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Feeling thermometer towards debtors as a group",
             
              coef_rename = coef_rename, notes= note_add("coded 1-100, higher values represent warmer feelings")
            ) %>% add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3))                %>% kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))
              


```

```{r tabguess}


model <- lm(debt_guess~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+newsint,data = cces_clean)
modelsummary(model, stars = TRUE, gof_omit = gof_omit, output = "latex",
             title = "Percent of debtors respondent guessed were first generation students",
             coef_rename = c("dem_to_rep" = "Dem to Rep partisanship", 
                              "ideo5_new" = "Liberal to conservative ideology",
                              "not.white" = "Non-white",
                              "female" = "Female",
                              "educ" = "Education level",
                              "faminc_new" = "Family income",
                              "student.debtor" = "Student debtor",
                              "newsint" = "Attention to news"))
```

```{r}
models <- list(
  
  "All"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treated,data = high_guessers),

  
    "All"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treated,data = high_guessers)
  
)

modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Support for canceling debt",
             
             
             coef_rename = c("dem_to_rep" = "Dem to Rep partisanship", 
                              "ideo5_new" = "Liberal to conservative ideology",
                              "not.white" = "Non-white",
                              "female" = "Female",
                              "educ" = "Education level",
                              "faminc_new" = "Family income",
                              "student.debtor" = "Student debtor",
                              "newsint" = "Attention to news",
                              "relief_first" = "Asked about relief first",
                              "relief_first:treated" = "Treatment x Relief order",
                              "treated" = "Treatment condition")
             
             
             
             
             ) %>% add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3)) %>% 
              kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))

```

```         
```

```{r}
models <- list(
  
                "All"=lm(debt_relief~treated*relief_first,data=cces_clean),
  
                "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~treated*relief_first,data=high_guessers),
               
               "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~treated*relief_first,data=low_guessers),
               
               
               "All"=lm(debt_relief~treated,data=cces_clean),
  
                "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~treated,data=high_guessers),
               
               "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~treated,data=low_guessers)
               
               )
               
#modelplot_custom(models)

#modelsummary(models)

modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Support for debt relief (Matched Data)",
             
              coef_rename = c("treated" = "Treated with new information",
                              "relief_first" = "Asked about relief first",
                              "treated:relief_first" = "Treatment x Relief first")
             
             
             
             ) %>%add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3))%>% kable_styling(latex_options="scale_down","striped")  
             

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-minGraph
#| fig-cap: "Marginal Means for both outcomes"
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| results: hide
#There can ONLY BE ONE FIGURE PER CHUNK IF YOU WANT THE LABEL TO WORK

responsibility_mm <- plot_mm(conjoint,responsibility_formula)+theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = "black", linetype = "solid"))+ labs(caption = "Debtors Bearing Responsibility")+ scale_y_discrete(labels = function(labels) gsub("treat_", "", labels))

cancel_mm <- plot_mm(conjoint,cancel_formula)+theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank(),

    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = "black", linetype = "solid"))+ labs(caption = "Support for cancelation") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

library(gridExtra) 
combined_plot <- grid.arrange(responsibility_mm, cancel_mm, ncol = 2)

combined_plot
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-mmpartisanresponsible
#| fig-cap: "Marginal means by partisanship"
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| results: hide

responsible_mm_partisan <- cj(conjoint, debtors_not_responsible ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "mm", by = ~Partisan) %>% filter(Partisan!="Other/Indepdent")




responsible_mm_partisan <- plot(responsible_mm_partisan, group = "Partisan", vline = 0)+theme_tufte()+scale_color_few()+theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
             panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = "black", linetype = "solid")                                                       
        )+ labs(caption = "Debtors Bearing Responsibility")+
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(labels) gsub("treat_", "", labels))+ theme(legend.position = "none")


cancel_mm_partisan <- cj(conjoint, debt_cancel ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "mm", by = ~Partisan) %>% filter(Partisan!="Other/Indepdent")

cancel_mm_partisan <- plot(cancel_mm_partisan, group = "Partisan", vline = 0)+theme_tufte()+scale_color_few()+theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),

        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = "black", linetype = "solid"))+ labs(caption = "Support for cancelation") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5))


combined_plot <- grid.arrange(responsible_mm_partisan, cancel_mm_partisan, ncol = 2)

combined_plot


```

```         
```

```{r}
mm_all_cancel <- cj(conjoint, debt_cancel ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "mm") %>% mutate (Partisan = "All")

cancel_mm_partisan <- cj(conjoint, debt_cancel ~ treat_biden + treat_school +treat_finish + treat_race +treat_working, estimate = "mm", by = ~Partisan) %>% filter(Partisan!="Other/Indepdent") %>% select(-BY)


test <- rbind(mm_all_cancel,cancel_mm_partisan)
#View(test)


library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Reshape data to long format
data_long <- test %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = estimate:upper, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  select(Partisan, feature, level, metric, value)

# Calculate mean for each combination of Partisan, feature, and level
means <- data_long %>%
  group_by(Partisan, feature, level, metric) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Spread the means to wide format
table_data <- means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = mean_value)

# Print the table
tab_data <- table_data %>%
  select(Partisan,level, estimate, `std.error`, z, p, lower, upper) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Partisan, values_from = c(estimate, `std.error`, z, p, lower, upper)) %>%
  rename(`Level` = level) %>%
  select(`Level`, starts_with("estimate"), starts_with("std.error"), starts_with("z"), starts_with("p"), starts_with("lower"), starts_with("upper")) #%>%
  
  #print(row.names = FALSE)

tab_data2 <- tab_data %>% 
  mutate(
    Condition= Level,
    
    All=paste0(round(estimate_All,2)," (",round(std.error_All,3),")"),
    
    Democrats =paste0(round(estimate_Democrat,2)," (",round(std.error_Democrat,3),")"),
    
    Republicans =paste0(round(estimate_Republican,2)," (",round(std.error_Republican,3),")")
    
  ) %>% select(Condition,All,Democrats, Republicans)

tab_data2 %>%   kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE,caption = "Marginal Means by subgroup, Support for debt cancelation") %>%kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))#%>%
#kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, width = "15cm")  #kable_styling(font_size = 8) %>%
 # column_spec(column = 1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  #escape(lineheight = 0.2) %>%
 # row_spec(0, angle = 0)

```

```{r tableAMCEcancel}



  
#library(kableExtra)

#library(kableExtra)

 #Assuming the bottom table is a data frame called 'bottom_table'
#bottom_table_formatted <- test %>%
  #rename(Characteristic = 1, Estimate = 2, `Std.Error` = 3, `z` = 4, `p` = 5, Lower = 6, Upper = 7) %>%
  #mutate(`P` = case_when(
    #`p` < 0.001 ~ "***",
    #`p` < 0.01 ~ "**",
    #`p` < 0.05 ~ "*",
    #TRUE ~ as.character(`p`)
  #)) %>%
 # select(-p,-Upper,-Lower) %>%
  #kbl(digits = c(0, 6, 6, 6, 0, 6, 6, 0), align = "c") %>%
 # kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left") %>%
 ## add_header_above(c( "   " = 1, "   " = 1, "   " = 1, "   " = 1, "   " = 1)) %>%
  #row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, align = "l") 

#bottom_table_formatted#custom_plot(full_sample_model_cancel) + 
  #labs(title = "Cancel Debt, Full Sample",caption = cancel_caption)

#ggsave("whole_sample_cancel.pdf")

```

```{r}
models <- list(
  
  "All"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = high_guessers),

  
    "All"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debtor_therm~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = high_guessers)
  
)

 modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Feeling thermometer towards debtors as a group",
             
              coef_rename = c("dem_to_rep" = "Dem to Rep partisanship", 
                              "ideo5_new" = "Liberal to conservative ideology",
                              "not.white" = "Non-white",
                              "female" = "Female",
                              "educ" = "Education level",
                              "faminc_new" = "Family income",
                              "student.debtor" = "Student debtor",
                              "newsint" = "Attention to news",
                              "relief_first" = "Asked about relief first",
                              "relief_first:treat_size" = "Treatment x Relief order",
                              "treat_size" = "Difference between guessed and actual")
             )%>%add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3)) %>%               kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))


```

```{r}
models <- list(
  
  "All"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+relief_first*treat_size,data = high_guessers),

  
    "All"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = cces_clean),

  "Low Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = low_guessers),

  "High Guessers"=lm(debt_relief~dem_to_rep+ideo5_new+not.white+female+educ+faminc_new+student.debtor+treat_size,data = high_guessers)
  
)

 modelsummary(models,output = "kableExtra",stars = TRUE,gof_omit = gof_omit,title = "Support for debt cancelation",
             
              coef_rename = c("dem_to_rep" = "Dem to Rep partisanship", 
                              "ideo5_new" = "Liberal to conservative ideology",
                              "not.white" = "Non-white",
                              "female" = "Female",
                              "educ" = "Education level",
                              "faminc_new" = "Family income",
                              "student.debtor" = "Student debtor",
                              "newsint" = "Attention to news",
                              "relief_first" = "Asked about relief first",
                              "relief_first:treat_size" = "Treatment x Relief order",
                              "treat_size" = "Difference between guessed and actual")
             )%>%add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "With Interaction" = 3, "Without Interaction" = 3)) %>%
              kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"))


```

# Supplementary Appendix

```{r}
#| label: tbl-tabmlogit
#| tbl-cap: "Multinomial Logit model of Treatment Assignment for CES Module"
#| echo: false

pacman::p_load(mlogit)
mlogit_data <- mlogit.data(cces_clean, choice = "treated", shape = "wide")
mlogit_model <- mlogit(treated ~ 1 | age + female + not.white + dem_to_rep + college.grad +faminc_new, data = mlogit_data)
modelsummary(mlogit_model,
             output = "kableExtra",
             stars = TRUE,
             gof_omit = gof_omit,
             coef_rename = c("age" = "Age",
                             "female" = "Female",
                             "faminc_new" = "Income",
                             "college.grad"="College Graduate",
                             "dem_to_rep"="Partisanship (Dem to Rep)",
                             "not.white"="Non White")) %>% 
  kable_styling(latex_options="scale_down", "striped")
```
